2604.15802.title: CHOP: Chunkwise Context-Preserving Framework for RAG on Multi Documents
2604.15802.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems lose retrieval accuracy when similar documents coexist in the vector database, causing unnecessary information, hallucinations, and factual errors. To alleviate this issue, we propose CHOP, a framework that iteratively evaluates chunk relevance with Large Language Models (LLMs) and progressively reconstructs documents by determining their association with specific topics or query types. CHOP integrates two key components: the CNM-Extractor, which generates compact per-chunk signatures capturing categories, key nouns, and model names, and the Continuity Decision Module, which preserves contextual coherence by deciding whether consecutive chunks belong to the same document flow. By prefixing each chunk with context-aware metadata, CHOP reduces semantic conflicts among similar documents and enhances retriever discrimination. Experiments on benchmark datasets show that CHOP alleviates retrieval confusion and provides a scalable approach for building high-quality knowledge bases, achieving a Top-1 Hit Rate of 90.77% and notable gains in ranking quality metrics.
2604.15802.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）系統在向量數據庫中存在相似文檔時會失去檢索準確性，導致不必要的信息、幻覺和事實錯誤。為了緩解這一問題，我們提出了CHOP，一個通過大型語言模型（LLMs）迭代評估塊相關性的框架，並通過確定其與特定主題或查詢類型的關聯來逐步重建文檔。CHOP整合了兩個關鍵組件：CNM-Extractor，該組件生成緊湊的每個塊簽名，以捕捉類別、關鍵名詞和模型名稱，以及連續性決策模塊，該模塊通過決定連續塊是否屬於同一文檔流來保持上下文的一致性。通過在每個塊前加上上下文感知的元數據，CHOP減少了相似文檔之間的語義衝突，並增強了檢索器的區分能力。在基準數據集上的實驗表明，CHOP緩解了檢索混淆，並提供了一種可擴展的方法來構建高質量的知識庫，實現了90.77%的Top-1命中率和在排名質量指標上顯著的增益。
2604.15802.author: Hyunseok Park et.al.
2604.15802.authors: Hyunseok Park, Jihyeon Kim, Jongeun Kim, Dongsik Yoon
